description:
You are a log-analyst who must perform a very small, well-scoped SQLite investigation from the command line.

    Initial data available to you:
      • A directory called /home/user/logs already exists.  
      • Inside it there is a CSV file named events.csv that contains three columns (timestamp, severity, message) and eight data rows.

    Goal:
      1. Create a new SQLite database file called /home/user/logs/event_analysis.db.
      2. Inside that database create one table named event_log whose schema must exactly be:
           timestamp TEXT,
           severity  TEXT,
           message   TEXT
      3. Import every row from /home/user/logs/events.csv into that table.
      4. Produce a severity summary that lists how many rows belong to each distinct severity.  
         • The summary must be sorted first by the count in descending order and, for equal counts, by the severity value in alphabetical ascending order.  
         • The output must use SQLite’s default column separator “|” and have **no header line**.  
         • Each line must therefore look like:  SEVERITY|COUNT
      5. Redirect (save) the query output into a text file named /home/user/logs/severity_summary.log.

    Expected format of /home/user/logs/severity_summary.log
    -------------------------------------------------------
    ERROR|3
    INFO|3
    WARNING|2

    When you are done, the only new artefacts in /home/user/logs/ should be:
      • event_analysis.db  – the SQLite database file.
      • severity_summary.log – the text file that contains exactly the three lines shown above, terminated with a trailing newline.

    Hints (not commands):
      • Use the sqlite3 CLI that is pre-installed in the container.
      • Most of the task can be achieved with three or four commands: opening sqlite3 while pointing to the DB, creating the table, importing the CSV, running the aggregate query with ORDER BY, and redirecting its result to the required log file.
truth:
The file /home/user/logs/events.csv is present before the agent begins and contains exactly:

timestamp,severity,message
2023-07-10 10:00:00,INFO,Service started
2023-07-10 10:05:00,WARNING,High memory usage
2023-07-10 10:10:00,ERROR,Service crashed
2023-07-10 10:15:00,INFO,Service restarted
2023-07-10 10:20:00,ERROR,Missing config file
2023-07-10 10:25:00,WARNING,Disk near capacity
2023-07-10 10:30:00,INFO,Health check ok
2023-07-10 10:35:00,ERROR,Service crashed

    After the agent finishes, automated verification will confirm:

    1. /home/user/logs/event_analysis.db exists and is a valid SQLite database.
    2. Querying the database with:
           sqlite3 /home/user/logs/event_analysis.db "SELECT severity, COUNT(*) FROM event_log GROUP BY severity ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, severity ASC;"
       returns:
           ERROR|3
           INFO|3
           WARNING|2
    3. /home/user/logs/severity_summary.log exists, is readable, and its entire content (including a trailing newline) is:

ERROR|3
INFO|3
WARNING|2
name: task_000000_176d0aa4
